quantification.why: Essentially every comparison carries a computed figure (e.g., "out-earned Widget by 12.9%", "53.8% ahead of West's", "separated by only 1.4 points while the North-to-West gap spans 10.6 points").

specificity.why: Each recommendation names a product/region with data ("Grow Gadget sales: only 16 units produced 33.4% of revenue"; "Bundle low-priced Sprockets... drove 63.1% of units sold but only 15.8% of revenue"; "Investigate West, the weakest region at 19.8%").

insight_density.why: Multiple actionable, non-obvious observations surface — the median-mean skew from large orders, Sprocket's volume-vs-revenue mismatch ("63.1% of all 377 units sold" but 15.8% revenue), and the 1/31 price ratio driving it.

quantification.why: Essentially every comparison carries a computed figure — e.g., 'Gadget out-earned Widget by 12.9%', 'North...53.8% ahead of West's', and 'separated by only 1.4 points while the North-to-West gap spans 10.6 points'.

specificity.why: Every recommendation names products/regions with figures, e.g., 'Bundle low-priced Sprockets...they drove 63.1% of units sold but only 15.8% of revenue' and 'Investigate West, the weakest region at 19.8% of revenue.'

insight_density.why: Multiple non-obvious, actionable observations surface, including the Sprocket volume/revenue mismatch (63.1% of units, 15.8% of revenue), Gadget's revenue concentration (16 units = 33.4%), and the median sitting 14.1% below the mean due to large orders.
